2604.20652.title: Large Language Models Outperform Humans in Fraud Detection and Resistance to Motivated Investor Pressure
2604.20652.paper_summary: Large language models trained on human feedback may suppress fraud warnings when investors arrive already persuaded of a fraudulent opportunity. We tested this in a preregistered experiment across seven leading LLMs and twelve investment scenarios covering legitimate, high-risk, and objectively fraudulent opportunities, combining 3,360 AI advisory conversations with a 1,201-participant human benchmark. Contrary to predictions, motivated investor framing did not suppress AI fraud warnings; if anything, it marginally increased them. Endorsement reversal occurred in fewer than 3 in 1,000 observations. Human advisors endorsed fraudulent investments at baseline rates of 13-14%, versus 0% across all LLMs, and suppressed warnings under pressure at two to four times the AI rate. AI systems currently provide more consistent fraud warnings than lay humans in an identical advisory role.
2604.20652.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型在訓練過程中接受人類反饋，可能會在投資者已經被說服進入詐騙機會時抑制詐騙警告。我們在一項預註冊的實驗中測試了這一點，涵蓋七個主要的LLM和十二個投資情境，包括合法、高風險和客觀詐騙的機會，結合了3360次AI諮詢對話和1201名參與者的人類基準。與預測相反，動機驅動的投資者框架並未抑制AI詐騙警告；如果有的話，反而略微增加了詐騙警告。在不到千分之三的觀察中發生了背書反轉。人類顧問在基線下對詐騙投資的背書率為13-14%，而所有LLM的背書率為0%，在壓力下的警告抑制率是AI的兩到四倍。目前，AI系統在相同的諮詢角色中提供的詐騙警告比普通人類更為一致。
2604.20652.author: Nattavudh Powdthavee et.al.
